reasoning: The statistical model from the FRED historical data provides a 0.30 probability of the real interest rate increasing, as the rate has exhibited a consistent downward trend over the past year (dropping from ~2.05% in January to 1.608% in November). I anchor on a ~0.34 probability for the first resolution date (1-month horizon) to reflect this documented negative momentum while adjusting slightly upward to account for a recent tick upward in the November reading. Given that interest rates exhibit random walk characteristics and mean reversion over longer periods, the purely linear trend is less reliable for the 3-month horizon. Therefore, my uncertainty increases for the later resolution date. In accordance with forecasting principles regarding extending horizons, the prediction for February 2026 is pulled closer to 0.5, landing at 0.44.
